annotations.label: Associative property

anchor.snippet: the associative property

annotations.note: The associative law `(a*b)*c = a*(b*c)` is the field of `Semigroup` and the theorem `mul_assoc`.

annotations.label: Associative binary operation

anchor.snippet: is called associative if it satisfies the associative law

annotations.note: `Semigroup` bundles a multiplication satisfying associativity (and `Std.Associative` exists as an unbundled predicate).

annotations.label: Generalized associative law

anchor.snippet: This is called the generalized associative law

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state a `n`-ary bracketing theorem explicitly, but in any `Semigroup`/`Monoid` it is realized via the well-definedness of `List.prod` and lemmas like `List.prod_append`.

annotations.label: Number of bracketings is Catalan number

anchor.snippet: The number of possible bracketings is just the Catalan number

annotations.note: The count of binary trees with `n` internal nodes (i.e. bracketings of `n+1` symbols) equals `catalan n`.

annotations.label: Biconditional bracketing ambiguity

anchor.snippet: An example where this does not work is the logical biconditional

annotations.note: The pragmatic notational ambiguity of chained `↔` (versus its formal associativity) is not a statement Mathlib formalizes.

annotations.label: String concatenation is associative

anchor.snippet: string concatenation is associative

annotations.note: No `String.append_assoc` lemma is provided in Mathlib (`String` is a core type and Mathlib provides no `Semigroup String` instance).

annotations.label: Addition and multiplication of real numbers are associative

anchor.snippet: addition and multiplication of real numbers are associative

annotations.note: `Real` has a `Field ℝ` instance, so `add_assoc` and `mul_assoc` instantiate on the reals.

annotations.label: Trivial projection operations are associative

anchor.snippet: The trivial operation x ∗ y = x

annotations.note: Mathlib does not name the `x ∗ y = x` left-projection semigroup as an instance or example.

annotations.label: Complex numbers, quaternions, octonions

anchor.snippet: Addition and multiplication of complex numbers and quaternions are associative

annotations.note: `Complex` is a field and `Quaternion R` is a `Ring`, so associativity is inherited; however, octonions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: gcd and lcm are associative

anchor.snippet: The greatest common divisor and least common multiple functions act associatively

annotations.note: `gcd_assoc` and `lcm_assoc` give associativity of `gcd` and `lcm` in a `NormalizedGCDMonoid`.

annotations.label: Intersection and union of sets

anchor.snippet: Taking the intersection or the union of sets

annotations.note: `Set.inter_assoc` and `Set.union_assoc` give associativity of `∩` and `∪` on `Set α`.

annotations.label: Function composition is associative

anchor.snippet: the operation of function composition on S is associative

annotations.note: `Function.comp_assoc` (used throughout Mathlib) establishes associativity of `∘`.

annotations.label: Composition of maps between sets

anchor.snippet: composition of maps is always associative

annotations.note: Same `Function.comp_assoc` covers associativity for arbitrary maps between sets.

annotations.label: Composition of morphisms in a category

anchor.snippet: composition of morphisms is associative by definition

annotations.note: `Category.assoc` is a field of the `Category` typeclass asserting `(f ≫ g) ≫ h = f ≫ (g ≫ h)`.

annotations.label: Three-element associative table

anchor.snippet: Consider a set with three elements

annotations.note: The specific 3-element multiplication table example from the article is not present as a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Matrix multiplication is associative

anchor.snippet: matrix multiplication is associative

annotations.note: `Matrix.mul_assoc` proves associativity of matrix multiplication, used to build the semiring instance on square matrices.

annotations.label: Min and max are associative

anchor.snippet: the minimum and maximum operation is associative

annotations.note: `min_assoc`/`max_assoc` (from the lattice `inf_assoc`/`sup_assoc`) and `Std.Associative max` give associativity of `min` and `max`.

annotations.label: Associativity rule of replacement

anchor.snippet: are two valid rules of replacement

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize proof-system replacement rules as syntactic schemata.

annotations.label: Associative truth-functional connectives

anchor.snippet: associativity is a property of particular connectives

annotations.note: Individual associativity lemmas like `and_assoc`, `or_assoc`, `iff_assoc` exist (mostly inherited from core), but there is no general meta-statement about associative connectives.

annotations.label: Joint denial is non-associative

anchor.snippet: Joint denial is an example of a truth functional connective that is not associative

annotations.note: NOR/joint-denial is not defined in Mathlib, so non-associativity of it is not stated.

annotations.label: Non-associative operation

anchor.snippet: that does not satisfy the associative law is called non-associative

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Mul` (binary operation without associativity) and `NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring`, but no named predicate `IsNonAssociative`.

annotations.label: Infinite sums are not associative

anchor.snippet: it is not associative inside infinite sums

annotations.note: No Mathlib theorem demonstrates non-associativity of `tsum` for conditionally convergent series.

annotations.label: Non-associative algebras: octonions and Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: Examples are the octonions and Lie algebras

annotations.note: `LieRing`/`LieAlgebra` are formalized as non-associative structures, but octonions are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Floating-point addition is non-associative

anchor.snippet: addition and multiplication of floating point numbers are not associative

annotations.note: `Mathlib.Data.FP.Basic` defines floating-point representations but does not prove non-associativity of `+`.

annotations.label: Left-associative operation

anchor.snippet: A left-associative operation is a non-associative operation that is conventionally evaluated from left to right

annotations.note: Left-associativity is a parser convention (`infixl`) rather than a mathematical definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Right-associative operation

anchor.snippet: a right-associative operation is conventionally evaluated from right to left

annotations.note: Right-associativity is likewise a notation/parser convention (`infixr`) and not a mathematical statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associativity implies commutativity under continuity and injectivity

anchor.section: Relationship with commutativity in certain special cases
anchor.snippet: Associative operators defined on an interval of the real number line are commutative if they are continuous and injective in both arguments

annotations.note: This theorem of associative+continuous+injective ⇒ commutative on a real interval is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strictly increasing continuous associative operators are commutative

anchor.section: Relationship with commutativity in certain special cases
anchor.snippet: every continuous, associative operator on two real inputs that is strictly increasing in each of its inputs is commutative

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement deduces commutativity from associativity plus continuity and strict monotonicity on the reals.
